data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Soaring Today, 4/8/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures surged late Tuesday night after President Donald Trump said he would delay planned attacks on Iran for two weeks. The move eased tensions in the five-week conflict and sent oil prices lower. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 3.15%, 2.25%, and 2.47%, respectively, at 12:30 a.m. EDT on April 8.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

As a result, crude prices moved lower, with Brent crude (CM:BZ) down 13.4% to $94.72 per barrel, while Brent crude (CM:CL) fell 14.24% to $94.8 per barrel.

In the regular trading session, U.S. stocks pared losses to finish mixed as President Donald Trump increased pressure on Iran ahead of his deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq each rose 0.1%, while the Dow slipped 0.2%.

Looking ahead, traders will turn their attention to the FOMC meeting minutes, set for release tomorrow. Markets use the minutes to gauge whether the Fed is leaning toward rate cuts, rate hikes, or staying on hold. On the earnings front, Delta Air Lines (DAL) will report its quarterly earnings on Wednesday.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Did Paramount Skydance (PSKY) Stock Gain 11% on April 7?
data.description:
Shares of Paramount Skydance (PSKY) gained 10.7% on April 7, after the David Ellison-led company confirmed backing from Middle East sovereign wealth funds for the Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) deal. In an 8-K filing, PSKY noted that Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF), Qatar Investment Authority (via an affiliate), and Abu Dhabi's L'imad Holding Co. have joined as equity syndication partners. While exact stakes remain undisclosed, earlier reports note their combined commitment at $24 billion, with PIF taking a roughly $10 billion stake.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The company noted that their agreement marks "an important milestone in the WBD transaction process, and that the resulting diversification of its shareholder base, the potential for strategic and commercial opportunities with the various Equity Syndication Parties, and the value of the Warrants described below, enhance long-term shareholder value."

Paramount Accelerates WBD Deal

Paramount has agreed to buy WBD for a massive $111 billion. It would combine Paramount's assets like CBS News, Paramount Pictures, and Paramount+ with WBD's HBO, Warner Bros. Pictures, CNN, TNT, TBS, and more. The deal is pending approval from WBD shareholders and regulators.

Paramount's 8-K filing identifies the equity syndication parties as affiliates of the Ellison family, including David Ellison and his father, Larry Ellison, alongside RedBird Capital Partners. The filing also stated that warrants issued to syndication investors, which allow them to buy PSKY stock, support the company's goal of expanding its public float over time. The PSKY shares for these investors are "non-voting."

The Ellison family and RedBird Capital will retain the largest equity stake and own all voting Class A Common Stock (100% of votes). Thus, executives do not anticipate any review from the Committee on Foreign Investment in the U.S. (CFIUS) or Federal Communications Commission (FCC).

Is PSKY a Good Investment?

On TipRanks, PSKY has a Moderate Sell consensus rating based on five Holds and five Sell ratings. The average Paramount Skydance price target of $11.38 implies 4.4% upside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, PSKY shares have dropped 18.2%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Intuitive Machines (LUNR) Rides NASA Momentum with a Long Growth Runway
data.description:
Intuitive Machines, Inc. (LUNR), a space infrastructure and services company headquartered in Houston, Texas, has become a key player in America's emerging lunar economy strategy. On March 25, Intuitive Machines' stock jumped about 15–16% after announcing the new NASA Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) contract, and added another roughly 17% on April 2 as investors cheered the successful Artemis II launch, which marked the first crewed Moon mission in over 50 years.

Intuitive Machines played a key role in the Artemis II launch by tracking the Orion spacecraft during its journey. Even after these gains, I believe Intuitive Machines still has significant room to grow, as its role in the lunar economy continues to expand. I remain bullish on LUNR.
data.author: Dilantha De Silva

data.title: Seagate (STX) vs. Western Digital (WDC): Morgan Stanley Picks Top IT Hardware Stock
data.description:
Five-star analyst Eric Woodring from Morgan Stanley just shook up his IT hardware rankings, picking Seagate (STX) as its top stock over longtime favorite Western Digital (WDC). While both companies stand to benefit from the AI and data storage boom, he believes Seagate offers stronger growth potential, making it the top IT hardware stock to watch. In addition, Woodring reiterated Buy ratings on both stocks and raised his price targets from $468 to $582 for STX stock and from $369 to $380 for WDC.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Trade WDC with leverage

Seagate and Western Digital Set to Benefit from Strong HDD Demand

Morgan Stanley's optimism on the sector is based on steady demand for hard disk drives (HDDs), which shows no signs of slowing. Woodring highlighted that cloud computing keeps growing, and AI is creating huge amounts of data that need storage. Right now, HDDs handle about 80% of global cloud storage.

He also stated that new AI applications and complex computing tasks are driving massive data growth, keeping HDD demand strong. The bank now expects the HDD market to reach supply-demand balance in 2029, a year later than previously forecast. Since only a few companies dominate this industry, this extended timeline is good news for Seagate and Western Digital.

On the flip side, flash memory is gradually taking market share from HDDs. However, Woodring believes the shift is slow.

Why Morgan Stanley Now Favors Seagate

Looking ahead, Woodring expects Seagate's gross profit margins to rise about 50 basis points above Western Digital's over the next year. This advantage comes from Seagate's quicker rollout of higher-capacity storage drives, which generate stronger profits. At the same time, many of the expected catalysts for Western Digital—such as selling its remaining SanDisk stake and closing the valuation gap with Seagate—have already taken place.

Woodring noted that the gap between Seagate and Western Digital is still modest. Overall, he remains highly confident in the HDD sector as compared to any other markets under his coverage.

STX or WDC: Which Is the Better Memory Stock, According to Analysts?

Using TipRanks' Comparison Tool, we compared STX and WDC to see which stock analysts favor. Both stocks carry a Strong Buy rating, though upside is modest after recent price surges. Seagate's average price target of $472.94 implies an upside of about 0.90%, while Western Digital's target of $327.78 suggests 5% potential gains.

Investors can dig deeper to decide which memory stock best fits their strategy. Below is a screenshot for reference.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Tesla Stock Gets a Boost from Intel's Partnership in Terafab AI Chip Project
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) stock jumped 4.3% in after-hours trading on April 7, after chip maker Intel (INTC) announced it would join Elon Musk's Terafab AI chip project alongside SpaceX, xAI, and Tesla. Intel also confirmed hosting the billionaire at its campus over the weekend.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Trade TSLA with leverage

In a post on X, Intel highlighted its ability to "design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale," which will accelerate Terafab's goal. The project aims to produce 1 terawatt per year of compute to advance AI and robotics.

Tesla Stock Price Movement

Tesla shares initially showed little reaction amid pressure from missed Q1 2026 delivery estimates. Analysts remain divided, with JPMorgan's Ryan Brinkman recently issuing a "Sell" rating and 58% downside potential. Year-to-date, TSLA shares have lost 22.9%.

The stock finally climbed on April 7, buoyed by broader market gains. Last night, the U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Looks Mispriced as Software despite AI Leadership
data.description:
I'm very bullish on Microsoft (MSFT) stock because it's trading below its 200-week moving average, even as the market is nervous about the software industry's future. I understand the concern, but given that these fears regarding software are built on the proliferation of artificial intelligence (AI) tools, I'm remaining stoutly confident on the future of MSFT stock because it's also a leader in AI as well as a software pioneer. That gives me growth and temporary value all in one.

data.title: UnitedHealth (UNH) vs. Humana (HUM): Which Healthcare Stock Is the Better Bet After the CMS Update?
data.description:
The healthcare sector got a boost after Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) finalized 2027 reimbursement rates. Medicare will raise payments to private insurers by 2.48%, higher than the near-flat increase proposed earlier. The update eases cost concerns and supports margins for Medicare Advantage plans. Shares of UnitedHealth (UNH) and Humana (HUM) both trended higher on the news, but investors are now weighing which stock offers better upside. Using the TipRanks Comparison tool, we take a closer look at which company is better positioned heading into 2026.

data.title: Gold Prices Hit 3-Week High after U.S.-Iran Ceasefire: 3 'Strong Buy' Gold Stocks to Buy Now
data.description:
Gold prices surged to a 3-week high during Asian trading on Wednesday, April 8, after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran. The U.S. dollar weakened, oil prices dropped over 15%, and safe haven demand lifted precious metals. At the time of writing, Spot Gold prices climbed 2.10% to $4,800 per ounce, while U.S. gold futures rose about 2.5% to $4,849 per ounce.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ahead of the 8 p.m. deadline set by Trump, he posted on Truth Social (DJT) that the U.S. would pause planned strikes on Iran's civilian infrastructure, such as bridges and power plants. The U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, conditional on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz for safe passage of vessels. At the same time, Israel and the U.S. must stop all hostilities against Iran.

Amid the chaos, investors can consider these 3 Strong Buy Gold stocks: B, NEM, and KGC, offering more than 30% upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: The Trade Desk Stock (TTD) Hits 52-Week Low on Triple Executive Exit and Analyst Downgrade
data.description:
The Trade Desk (TTD) stock fell to a fresh 52-week low on Tuesday, closing at $20.70, as leadership changes and a cautious analyst note shook investor confidence. The ad-tech company, once a strong performer, is now down nearly 77% from its all-time highs.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest drop comes as leadership changes and governance concerns raise questions about the company's near-term execution.

A "Triple Threat" of Leadership Departures

The biggest trigger for the sell-off was the exit of three top executives at the same time.

  • Chief Marketing Officer Ian Colley is leaving after seven years.
  • Consumer Products head Matthew Henick, who led the Ventura project, is also stepping down.
  • Communications head Melinda Zurich is exiting as well.

Wells Fargo Turns Cautious on Execution Risks

Following the news, Wells Fargo lowered its price target on TTD to $24 from $25 while maintaining an Equal Weight rating.

The move signals growing caution around how the company will execute during this transition period. Investors are particularly focused on whether The Trade Desk can continue to build out its Ventura ecosystem, which is meant to compete in the connected TV space.

Is TTD Stock Still a Buy?

Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on The Trade Desk stock based on 13 Buy, 16 Hold, and three Sell recommendations. The average TTD stock price target of $31.81 indicates 53.67% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Recover Losses as U.S.-Iran Weigh 2-Week Ceasefire ahead of Trump's Evening Deadline
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) reversed losses and closed slightly higher after Pakistan proposed a two-week ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Trump warned in a Truth Social post that Iran's "whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again." He added that a deal could be reached if Iran's new regime was willing to cooperate.
  • Following the post, Iran completely cut off direct communications with the U.S. At the same time, negotiations through mediators are still ongoing.
  • A U.S. official told Axios that optimism surrounding a deal has inched higher, with the attitude at the White House shifting from "can we get there?" to "can we get there by 8 o'clock tonight?"
  • Later in the day, Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said negotiations are "progressing steadily" and requested a two-week deadline extension, along with a two-week ceasefire and reopening of Hormuz as a gesture of goodwill.
  • White House spokesperson Karoline Leavitt said Trump will soon provide a response to Sharif's proposal, while a senior Iranian official said Iran is reviewing the proposal.
  • U.S. consumers now expect year-ahead inflation of 3.4%, up from 3.0% in February.
  • Apple (AAPL) closed lower following reports that its foldable iPhone had run into engineering issues during early testing and could be delayed. However, AAPL recovered some of its midday losses after Bloomberg reported that the smartphone was still on track for a September release.
  • Home Depot (HD) closed lower after Jim Cramer said he was "very disappointed" in the home improvement company, citing high mortgage rates and its involvement with ICE.
  • Anthropic announced a multibillion dollar AI chip deal with Google (GOOGL) and Broadcom (AVGO).

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Communication services was the best-performing sector on Tuesday with Paramount Skydance (PSKY) posting a 10% return following news that three Middle Eastern sovereign-wealth funds would back its acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). The sector also benefited from a market-wide recovery as investors shifted capital into risk-on assets.

Several communication services stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Paramount Skydance (PSKY)
  • Google (GOOGL)
  • Charter Communications (CHTR)
  • T-Mobile (TMUS)
  • Take-Two Interactive (TTWO)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the consumer staples sector faced the largest drawdown as investors rotated out of defensive names amid growing optimism for a U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Campbell's (CPB) led to the downside on fears that it could be removed from the S&P 500 (SPX).

Notable consumer staples stocks trading lower include:

  • Campbell's (CPB)
  • Dollar Tree (DLTR)
  • Kimberly-Clark (KMB)
  • Walmart (WMT)
  • Kenvue (KVUE)
data.author: Eddie Pan
